_meta.title: Pressure Canning Verifier - Tested Process Whitelist
_meta.principle: The jar is a whitelist, not a calculator. In-jar processing is only valid when the jar contents map onto an already-tested USDA process. Anything not on this list is NOT a validated in-jar candidate and must be routed to freeze/vacuum-seal (Pathway 2) or fresh-at-serving (Pathway 3).
_meta.source: USDA Complete Guide to Home Canning (2015 revision) and its extension-service adaptations (NCHFP, OSU, UMaine, UNH, Cornell, NDSU). Times cross-verified across sources.
_meta.safety_note: These times are for reference-building only. Always confirm the exact process against the current USDA Complete Guide or the National Center for Home Food Preservation (nchfp.uga.edu) before canning. This data does not certify any recipe as safe.

basePressure.note: Base values apply at 0-2000 ft (dial) / 0-1000 ft (weighted). Use altitude adjustment.

pathway1_inJar._note: Times are minutes at base pressure. 'pt' = pints/half-pints, 'qt' = quarts. null = not recommended for that jar size. Half-pint = pint time; 1.5-pint = quart time.

meat_redMeat.displayName: Red meat - strips, cubes, or chunks (beef, pork, lamb, veal, venison, bear, goat)
meat_redMeat.prep: Trim gristle and excess fat. Cut into pieces. Hot pack: cook until rare (about two-thirds done), pack with boiling broth/water/tomato juice. Raw pack: pack raw, add no liquid.
meat_redMeat.fatRule: Remove excess fat before canning; fat can climb the jar and prevent a proper seal.

meat_ground.displayName: Ground or chopped meat (patties, balls, crumbles, cased sausage)
meat_ground.prep: Shape into patties/balls or saute loose until lightly browned. Drain excess fat. Pack with boiling broth/water/tomato juice. Coarse grind only - never puree.
meat_ground.safetyBlock: Do NOT can pureed/paste-textured meat - no tested process; heat cannot be guaranteed to reach the center.

poultry_boneless.displayName: Poultry / rabbit - without bones (chicken, turkey, rabbit)
poultry_boneless.prep: Can with or without bones. Boneless uses this time. Hot pack: cook until about two-thirds done. Remove excess fat.

poultry_bonein.prep: Bone-in pieces process shorter than boneless.

stock_broth.prep: Simmer bones, strain, cool, skim and discard fat, reheat to boiling, fill jars. Plain stock only - no thickeners.

seafood_fish.prep: Pints/half-pints only. Quarts NOT recommended for fish. Follow the specific USDA fish procedure exactly.

veg_mixed.prep: USDA tested mix: carrots, whole-kernel corn, cut green beans, lima beans, crushed tomatoes, cubed zucchini in roughly equal parts. Substitutions change the process.

veg_mushrooms.prep: Half-pints/pints only. WHOLE or SLICED only - this is the tested form. Domestic only, NEVER wild. Powdered/dehydrated mushroom is NOT a tested in-jar item (route to Pathway 2).

veg_peppers.prep: Half-pints/pints. Follow the specific USDA pepper procedure.

veg_potatoes.prep: Cube or use small whole. NEVER mashed or pureed.

veg_pumpkin_squash_cubed.prep: 1-inch CUBES only. NEVER mashed or pureed.

veg_sweet_potato.prep: Pieces only. NEVER mashed or pureed.

veg_beans_dried.prep: Must be fully soaked AND boiled 30 min before packing. If used in soup instead, rehydrate but do not pre-boil 30 min.

tomato_crushed.acidRule: MUST add bottled lemon juice (2 tbsp/qt, 1 tbsp/pt) or citric acid (1/2 tsp/qt, 1/4 tsp/pt). This time is the pressure-canner option.

soupGate.when: Two or more tested Pathway-1 foods combined with liquid in one jar.
rules: Every solid ingredient must itself have a tested canning recommendation (i.e. appear in pathway1_inJar). No cabbage, no broccoli, no cured/brined meats.
Fill jar HALFWAY with solids, then cover with liquid (broth/water/tomatoes), 1-inch headspace. The 1:1 solid-to-liquid ratio is a safety requirement, not a preference.
Boil the combined mixture 5 minutes before packing.
Meat must be pre-cooked before canning.
Dried beans/peas must be fully rehydrated first.
DO NOT thicken (no flour, cornstarch, Clearjel, or pureeing for body).
If seafood is present, the whole jar processes at the seafood time (100 min).

notInJar._note: These never go in the jar. 'route' tells the app which pathway to send them to.

hardBlock_thickeners.reason: Thickeners slow heat penetration to the jar core, causing under-processing (botulism risk).
hardBlock_thickeners.clearjelException: Modified starch (Clearjel) is safe ONLY inside a specific tested recipe that calls for it by name and amount. It is NOT permitted in the free-choice soup pathway.

hardBlock_dairy.reason: Low-acid, not safe to can; curdles/separates and can cause seal failure and spoilage.

hardBlock_starchGrains.reason: Swell and absorb liquid, turn to paste, and heat unevenly; no tested process.

hardBlock_eggs.reason: No research-tested process for home-canned eggs.

hardBlock_oilsFats.reason: Excess oil/fat insulates food (under-processing) and coats the seal (seal failure).
hardBlock_oilsFats.allowance: Up to ~1 tbsp added fat/oil for browning is acceptable; drain the rest. Larger quantities and finishing oils go outside the jar.

hardBlock_curedMeats.reason: NCHFP soup guidance excludes cured/brined meats; only permitted inside a specific tested recipe.

hardBlock_untestedVeg.reason: No USDA-tested process; texture also degrades badly under pressure.

hardBlock_puree.reason: Pureed/mashed low-acid foods have NO tested process - heat cannot be guaranteed to reach the center.

hardBlock_concentrates.reason: Concentrated/dehydrated low-acid flavorings have no tested in-jar process and add uneven density. Use them to build a Pathway-2 sauce.

hardBlock_alcohol.reason: Wine/spirits are not an approved jar-covering liquid. Simmer into a Pathway-2 sauce (which also mellows the raw alcohol).

hardBlock_condiments.reason: Condiments/emulsions - add fresh at serving, not in the jar.

seasonings_noCount._note: Flavor only, no safety impact, do not count toward jar-fill percentage. Dry spices/dried herbs may be added freely; salt is optional (flavor only, not a preservative).
seasonings_noCount.aromaticsNote: Fresh onion, garlic, and peppers are permitted as minor components inside a tested soup or tested recipe, but have limited/no standalone in-jar process on their own (peppers excepted, above).
